commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This member-authored PR needs maintainer acceptance of compatibility and ranking semantics, not an automated repair lane.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes authenticated telemetry handling and tests without new dependency or permission expansion. commands.review_summary:
The PR moves install telemetry from sync snapshots to explicit install events, makes legacy sync telemetry a no-op, removes installs from recommended/search ranking signals, adds a close install/download ratio abuse signal, and updates schema, docs, specs, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a telemetry/ranking product PR rather than a bug report. The changed behavior is reviewable from the diff and the built CLI request-capture tests described in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This member-authored PR needs maintainer acceptance of compatibility and ranking semantics, not an automated repair lane.

target.title: Update install telemetry and ranking signals
target.body:
## Summary This PR makes install counts come from explicit install events, keeps `clawhub sync` out of install telemetry, removes installs from ranking popularity, and extends Patrick's existing temporal abuse scan with a high-volume install/download close-ratio signal. It also rejects the old sync-shaped telemetry payload on the install telemetry endpoint. The deprecated `/api/cli/telemetry/sync` endpoint remains as a compatibility no-op for old clients and schema consumers, but sync snapshots are not counted as install events. ## Live Evidence This PR now includes artifact tests that build the CLI, run the built `bin/clawhub.js`, and point it at a real local HTTP server. The tests assert the actual HTTP requests seen by that server. `clawhub install demo --version 1.0.0` sends install telemetry: ```text GET /api/v1/skills/demo GET /api/v1/skills/demo/versions/1.0.0 GET /api/v1/download?slug=demo&version=1.0.0 POST /api/cli/telemetry/install ``` The recorded telemetry body is asserted as: ```ts { event: "install", slug: "demo", version: "1.0.0", rootId: expect.any(String), rootLabel: expect.stringContaining("skills") } ``` `clawhub sync --dry-run --json` does not send install telemetry: ```text GET /api/v1/whoami GET /api/v1/resolve?slug=demo&hash=... ``` The sync artifact test asserts no request path starts with `/api/cli/telemetry/`. ## 1. Install telemetry now comes from `clawhub install` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["User runs
clawhub install <slug>"] --> B["CLI installs skill"] B --> C["Best-effort telemetry POST"] C --> D["{ event: 'install', slug, version, rootId, rootLabel }"] D --> E["Convex records per-user install"] E --> F["Aggregate install counters update"] C -. "failure is swallowed" .-> G["Install command still succeeds"] ``` What changed: - `clawhub install` sends one best-effort `install` event. - The event includes `slug`, optional `version`, optional `rootId`, and optional `rootLabel`. - Install telemetry failure does not break local install. - Install counts shown in ClawHub now come from this explicit event path. ## 2. `clawhub sync` no longer reports installs ```mermaid flowchart TD A["clawhub sync"] --> B["Scan local publish roots"] B --> C["Compare local skills to registry"] C --> D["Publish / update catalog entries"] C -. "removed" .-> E["Install telemetry snapshot"] E -. "not an install event" .-> F["No install counters updated"] G["Old client sends { roots: [...] }
to sync telemetry endpoint"] --> H["Server returns ok"] H --> I["Compatibility no-op"] ``` What changed: - `clawhub sync` remains a publishing/catalog command. - Sync no longer sends install telemetry. - `/api/cli/telemetry/sync` remains as a compatibility no-op. - `/api/cli/telemetry/install` rejects old sync-shaped `roots` payloads. - Bare logged-in `clawhub` remains a sync shortcut. - `sync --bump` rejects invalid values before scanning. ## 3. Ranking popularity ignores installs ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Search / recommended candidate"] --> B["Lexical + semantic match"] B --> C["Popularity boost"] C --> D["Stars"] C --> E["Downloads"] C -. "removed" .-> F["Installs"] D --> G["Final rank"] E --> G ``` What changed: - Search/recommended popularity uses stars and downloads. - Installs are accepted in some call shapes for compatibility, but ignored by ranking. - This keeps install counts as a displayed metric, not a popularity-rank driver. ## 4. Close install/download ratio abuse signal Patrick's merged PR #2531 already catches high-download, flat-install behavior. This PR adds the opposite high-volume case: installs tracking downloads too closely. ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Temporal scan candidate"] --> B["Compute 7d and 30d windows"] B --> C{"Enough volume?"} C -- "7d downloads >= 1,000
or 30d downloads >= 2,000" --> D{"Enough installs?"} C -- "no" --> Z["No close-ratio signal"] D -- "installs >= 500" --> E{"Install/download ratio >= 50%?"} D -- "no" --> Z E -- "yes" --> F{"Excess z-score >= 50?"} E -- "no" --> Z F -- "yes" --> G["Flag temporal_installs_track_downloads"] F -- "no" --> Z ``` What changed: - Adds `nearConversion` to temporal abuse scoring. - Persists evidence fields: - `installDownloadRatio7` - `installDownloadRatio30` - `installDownloadExcessZScore7` - `installDownloadExcessZScore30` - Uses high thresholds so low-volume ratios do not matter. - Extends Patrick's temporal scan instead of replacing it. ## 5. Publisher abuse flow after this PR ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Publisher skill daily stats"] --> B{"Official org publisher?"} B -- "yes" --> C["Excluded from publisher abuse scoring"] B -- "no" --> D["Patrick #2531 signals"] D --> E["High downloads + flat installs"] B -- "no" --> F["This PR signal"] F --> G["High installs close to downloads"] E --> H["Publisher abuse review evidence"] G --> H ``` Scope notes: - Official org publishers remain excluded from publisher abuse scoring. - The GitHub-synced publisher display/ranking question is paused for this PR. - Patrick's high-download/flat-install signal remains useful as a guardrail, but downloads are already hard to inflate because metrics dedupe by target, identity, and day. ## 6. Schema, docs, and tests updated ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Telemetry behavior"] --> B["HTTP handler tests"] A --> C["CLI command tests"] A --> D["Telemetry mutation tests"] A --> K["Built CLI artifact tests
against local HTTP server"] E["Abuse scoring behavior"] --> F["Publisher abuse scoring tests"] E --> G["Publisher abuse persistence tests"] H["API shape"] --> I["Schema package build"] H --> J["Docs updates"] ``` What changed: - Adds telemetry tests for the explicit install event path. - Updates HTTP handler tests to reject sync-shaped roots payloads on the install endpoint and accept legacy sync telemetry as a no-op. - Adds live artifact tests that run the built CLI against a real local registry. - Adds artifact coverage for bare logged-in `clawhub` sync compatibility and early `sync --bump` validation. - Updates publisher abuse scoring tests for close install/download ratios. - Updates schema output and docs for the new telemetry model. ## Validation Latest after final sync-telemetry cleanup: - `bunx vitest run convex/httpApi.handlers.test.ts convex/telemetry.test.ts` - `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test:src -- src/cli/commands/skills.test.ts src/cli/commands/sync.test.ts` - `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test:artifact` - `bun run --cwd packages/schema build` - `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` - `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` - `git diff --check` Earlier on this PR: - `bunx convex codegen` - `bunx vitest run convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts --testNamePattern "high-volume installs|statistically elevated|30-day close-ratio|observed high-end"` - `bunx vitest run convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts` - `env -u ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME -u ARTIFACTORY_PASSWORD NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY=https://registry.npmjs.org BUN_CONFIG_REGISTRY=https://registry.npmjs.org bun run ci:static` - `bun run ci:unit` - `bun run ci:types-build` - `bun run ci:packages` - `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` Known local note: - `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` currently fails on this machine with `convex/emailsNode.ts(6,24): error TS2307: Cannot find module 'resend' or its corresponding type declarations.`
